data.title: Jacobs to modernize large wastewater facility in Western US, generating renewable energy; awarded contract for upgrades at San José–Santa Clara RWF in California.
data.description: An upgraded facility in San José is set to convert waste into renewable energy. Jacobs, a design-build contractor (NYSE: J), has been chosen for the project at the San José–Santa Clara Regional Wastewater Facility (RWF) in California. The initiative aims to enhance waste transformation processes at the facility, contributing to renewable energy generation. The upgrades signify a step towards sustainable practices and resource utilization.

data.title: Fujifilm partners with Voicebrook to integrate voice-enabled reporting in Synapse Pathology system.
data.description: A partnership has been formed between FUJIFILM Healthcare Americas Corporation and Voicebrook, Inc. FUJIFILM is a major supplier of diagnostic imaging and enterprise imaging solutions, while Voicebrook is known for its pathology reporting solutions. The goal of this collaboration is to integrate Voicebrook's VoiceOver PRO reporting platform with Fujifilm's Synapse® Pathology, a pathology PACS. This integration aims to improve case management through advancements in image digitization. This announcement comes as FUJIFILM continues to expand its presence in the healthcare industry.

data.title: Always Best Care named No. 454 on Entrepreneur Franchise 500 list for steady growth and entrepreneur accessibility.
data.description: Always Best Care Senior Services, a prominent senior care franchise based in Rocklin, California, has been acknowledged for its consistent expansion and appeal to entrepreneurs. As of January 21, 2026, the franchise has secured the No. 454 spot on a reputable ranking list in the United States. This recognition underlines the franchise's success and accessibility, making it an attractive option for those interested in the senior care sector.

data.title: Curaleaf opens adult-use sales in its only Maine location, celebrating launch at Bangor dispensary. #Curaleaf #Maine #Cannabis
data.description: On January 21, 2026, Curaleaf Holdings, Inc., a prominent global supplier of consumer cannabis items, unveiled its sole adult-use store in Maine. The company, listed on TSX as CURA and on OTCQX as CURLF, marked this launch as a significant development.

data.title: Total Return Securities Fund's tender offer for 4 million shares expired, with over 12 million shares tendered. Accepted shares prorated at 98% NAV.
data.description: Today, the Total Return Securities Fund (NYSE: SWZ) disclosed that its tender offer, aiming to acquire up to 4 million of the Fund’s shares for cash, concluded at 5:00 p.m., New York City time, on January 20, 2026. The Equiniti Trust Company, LLC reported that a total of 12,035,603.471 shares were validly offered for purchase. Since this number exceeds the target shares, the Fund will proportionally accept shares for payment at a rate of 98% of the per share net asset value at the close of today's trading session on the New York Stock Exchange.

data.title: UnionPay discusses transforming cross-border payments with connectivity and AI at the World Economic Forum in Davos.
data.description: On January 21, 2026, in Davos, Switzerland, political and corporate figures convened for the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting 2026 with the theme "A Spirit of Dialogue." The event addresses pressing issues arising from increasing geopolitical uncertainty and challenges in global trade. The gathering brings together key individuals to analyze and discuss potential solutions for current world challenges.

data.title: GreenFirst gets $30 million term loan from Canada's Softwood Lumber Program via BMO to support operations amid market volatility.
data.description: In NORTH BAY, Ontario, GreenFirst Forest Products Inc. (TSX: GFP) disclosed that it secured a $30 million term loan through the Softwood Lumber Program launched by the Canadian government. The loan, facilitated by banking partner BMO, aims to bolster liquidity and sustain operations amidst the prevailing market uncertainty in the North American lumber industry. This financing initiative is designed to offer GreenFirst enhanced financial flexibility in navigating the challenging market conditions.

data.title: Seattle-based mega team, McConnell Group led by Sean McConnell, transitions from Compass to The Real Brokerage.
data.description: In Miami, prominent real estate professional Sean McConnell and the renowned McConnell Group, formerly known as the NK Team, have made a significant move from Compass to The Real Brokerage. This transition marks a notable shift in the industry, with McConnell and his team bringing their expertise and client base to the new firm. The move signifies a strategic decision by the brokerage to align with top talent, with McConnell leading the way. With this shift, The Real Brokerage stands to benefit from McConnell's established reputation and network, boosting their presence in the market. The move underscores the competitive nature of the real estate sector, as firms like The Real Brokerage aim to solidify their position with high-profile additions.

data.title: Schwab reports record 2025 results with net income of $2.5 billion in 4Q and $1.33 earnings per share.
data.description: In Westlake, Texas, Charles Schwab Corporation announced a net income of $2.5 billion for the fourth quarter. This translates to earnings per share of $1.33.

data.title: NemoVideo's new AI tool in San Jose cuts video production time drastically; released in Nov 2025, features conversational editing for faster results.
data.description: NemoVideo, a platform that debuted in November 2025 in San Jose, California, has unveiled a pioneering feature known as "Conversational Editing." This innovative interface empowers content creators and marketing professionals to craft high-quality video content using simple natural language instructions. The company is based in San Jose, California, and as of January 21, 2026...

data.title: Stock futures fell after S&P 500's worst session since Oct 2025. Dow, S&P, Nasdaq down. Johnson & Johnson earnings; Trump's Davos address in focus. Treasury bonds at 4.27% and 3.58%. Fed likely to keep interest rates unchanged. Dow -0.10%, S&P -0.12%, Nasdaq -0.32%. Johnson & Johnson premarket down 1.48%. Bond yields at 4.27% and 3.58%. Fed likely to keep rates unchanged. Dow -0.10%, S&P -0.12%, Nasdaq -0.32%. Johnson & Johnson premarket down 1.48%. Bond yields at 4.27% and 3.58%. Fed likely to keep rates unchanged. 95% likelihood.
data.description: U.S. stock futures fell after Tuesday's sharp sell-off, with the S&P 500 index dropping over 2% - its worst session since October 2025. President Trump's new trade stance towards Europe, threatening additional tariffs, caused risk-off sentiment. European officials warned of retaliation potentially affecting up to 25% of U.S. exports to Europe. Trump is set to discuss Greenland at the World Economic Forum in Davos. 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.27%, two-year bond at 3.58%. CME Group's FedWatch tool shows a 95% likelihood of unchanged interest rates. Dow Jones, S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, Russell 2000 indices all down. SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust down 0.056% at $677.20, Invesco QQQ Trust ETF down 0.21% at $606.78 in premarket. Johnson & Johnson reported earnings of $2.46 per share, beating revenue expectations at $24.6 billion. JNJ stock trend is strong.

data.title: The Mended Hearts, Inc. appoints Natalie Crawford as Executive Director effective January 1, 2026 to ensure a smooth leadership transition.
data.description: In Leesburg, Georgia, The Mended Hearts, Inc. (MHI) has announced the appointment of Natalie Crawford as the new executive director starting January 1, 2026. This move comes as part of a strategic leadership transition to guarantee ongoing stability and growth. Crawford will collaborate with current MHI CEO Al Voss over a year-long transition, partaking in a structured training program and gradually taking on leadership duties. After the transition period, Crawford will officially assume full leadership of MHI.
